rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ESCOBAR VS. ENVISION GENERAL CONTRACTING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Be Relieved As Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RICARDO ESCOBAR's Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel. Off calendar as untimely under CCP § 1005(b). The motion was filed on December 6, 2013, and apparently served by mail. Moving party only provided 15 court days notice. Moving party did not timely file proof of service for the motion. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. FROOTS SFC, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANTs KEVIN B. LANIGAN & SIAVASH NAZHAND's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Pursuant to CCP §473.5, the motion to set aside/vacate judgment and the motion to quash service of summons on defendant Lanigan and defendant Nazhand are granted. Plaintiff's proofs of service forms creates a rebuttable presumption that service was proper, but Defendants rebut the presumption by their declarations that they did not receive actual notice of the complaint, were not served, and that the persons purportedly served do not match descriptions of any occupants or themselves. Although the declarations are not artfully drafted, there is a statement under penalty of perjury under the laws and the state of California on the (unnumbered) page entitled "Actual Knowledge of the Lawsuit," which the court construes as applying to the two previous pages entitled "Declaration." Defendants prepared a proposed answer (with the heading "Exhibit A - attachment to Paragraph 11"), which denies the allegations of the complaint, in compliance with CCP 473.5(b), although the answer, when filed, must have a caption page with the case name and number and title of pleading, and otherwise conform to the California Rules of Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: NALL & COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. RSP PORTRERO GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT RUTHANN PIETROFESO's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. As to the allegation that cross-defendants insisted on using a less expensive form of sandblasting, the motion is denied; the Court cannot state on this record that this allegation is irrelevant. As to the allegations and prayer for relief regarding attorney fees, the motion to strike is also denied. A prevailing party may potentially be awarded attorneys' fees on an implied indemnity claim under CCP § 1021.6. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NALL & COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. RSP PORTRERO GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT RUTHANN PIETROFESO's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. The special demurrer on the grounds of uncertainty is not properly noticed, and could be overruled on this ground. In any event, the cross-complaint is not uncertain, and the demurrer on this basis is overruled. Cross-complainant Qualogy Construction, Inc. ("QCI") has alleged sufficient facts for Pietrofeso to determine what issues must be admitted or denied. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal. 4th 612, 616.) uncertain. As to the demurrers pursuant to CCP 430.10(e), these demurrers are overruled because QCI has alleged sufficient facts to state a claim for equitable indemnity, comparative indemnity, and declaratory relief. The fact that Pietrofeso was dismissed from the underlying complaint is not dispositive of whether she can be a cross-defendant in a cross-complaint for equitable indemnity or comparative indemnity. (See Gem Developers v. Hallcraft Homes of San Diego, Inc. (1989) 213 Cal. App. 3d 419, 428; American Motorcycle Association v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal. 3d 578, 584 ("a named defendant is authorized to file a cross-complaint against any person, whether already a party to the action or not, from whom the named defendant seeks to obtain total or partial indemnity").) QCI alleges that Pietrofeso personally directed QCI to use a particular sandblasting method to remove paint. (Cross-Complaint 14, 15, 16.) Pietrofeso's contention that she was a corporate officer is not a basis to sustain the demurrers. (See Frances T. v. Village Green Owners Assn. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 490, 504.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SUNDAY BAZAAR, INC. VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO BY AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition, Production Of Documents, And Site Inspection; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 2 - DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition, Production Of Documents, And Site Inspection; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Defendant's motion to compel is denied without prejudice. The Plaintiff has appeared for deposition and that portion of the motion has been formally withdrawn. Plaintiff agreed at the deposition to provide certain documents and shall do so on or before January 20, 2014. If documents are not produced the Defendant may renew its motion to compel. The parties shall meet and confer within ten days from service of a copy of this Order to establish an agreed site inspection date which shall be no later than February 14, 2014. If the inspection does not take place on the agreed date, Defendant may renew its motion. While there were multiple communications attempting to schedule the multiple proceedings referenced in the motion, it appears there was no specific and final "meet and confer" communication prior to the filing of the motion. For that reason Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO A VELASQUEZ VS. MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANTs RHINA GRANILLO, MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, INC. & RICARDO ALVA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, without leave to amend. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff's claims are time-barred. Plaintiff filed this lawsuit on January 4, 2013. As to the first, third and fourth causes of action, Plaintiff's claims are time-barred pursuant to Gov. Code § 12965(b). The Right-to-Sue Notice issued to Plaintiff is dated October 29, 2009. (RJN, Exhibit A.) These claims are time barred because they were not filed within one year of the issuance of the right to sue letter. Paul v. Goodwill Industries of Southern California (2011) 193 Cal. App. 4th 718, 225. As to the second, fifth and sixth causes of action, Plaintiff's claims are time-barred pursuant to Civil Code § 335.1. Plaintiff's injury occurred on September 17, 2009 when his employment was terminated. (First Am. Complaint, 12b) To the extent that Plaintiff relies on his previous lawsuit in this court, Velazquez v. Mission Language and Vocational School (Case CGC-10-502272), that entire action was dismissed, without prejudice, on October 9, 2012." 'A dismissal "without prejudice" necessarily means without prejudice to the filing of a new action on the same allegations, so long as it is done within the period of the appropriate statute of limitations. [Citations.]' [Citations.]" (Cardiff Equities, Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1541, 1550.) Plaintiff did not timely file his new action within the applicable statute of limitations period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Defendant's motion to compel and for sanctions is unopposed. The motion is granted to the following extent: The Defendant shall have 30 days from the date of service of this Order to respond (without objection) to the outstanding discovery. Sanctions in the sum of $350.00 is awarded and shall be paid within thirty days from the date of service of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ALLYSYN OVERTON VS. PATRICK LAKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 30, 2013, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT ALLYSYN OVERTON's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Transferred to Department 501, the Real Property Court, to be heard on January 30, 2014. (See SFLR 8.10.) Per SFLR 2.6B and 2.6C, moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and the operative cross-complaint to Department 501, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 30, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). =302/MJM
